name: Story Canvas
summary: Renders a single WebCase ({ id, title, description, code }) in component isolation — the docs story renderer ratified by #626 Fork 2 (docs examples ARE webcases). The isolated frame keeps the demoed component's styles and scripts from leaking into the docs chrome (and vice-versa), so one fixture serves both the conformance loop and the docs surface. Interaction-tests fold in as webcases carrying an interaction script; docs-only presentation needs (ordering, prose) are optional webcase fields, not a second artifact.

customElements.pattern: Autonomous custom element (<story-canvas>) owning the isolated render lifecycle
customElements.adoption: The element takes a WebCase via property (`case`) or a `case-id` attribute resolved against the page's webcases, and renders code into its isolated frame.

shadowDom.pattern: Shadow root for the story frame (title/description chrome); style-encapsulated from the host page
shadowDom.adoption: The frame chrome lives in shadow DOM; the case render target is isolated separately so the case's own styles don't bleed into the chrome.

iframeIsolation.pattern: Isolated browsing context for the case render target (the strongest style/script boundary)
iframeIsolation.adoption: The default isolation tier mounts the case `code` in a same-origin srcdoc iframe so a case's global CSS/JS can't leak into the docs page; a lighter shadow-only tier is the opt-in for trusted, pure-component cases.

oneFixtureTwoConsumers.description: The story-canvas renders the same WebCase the conformance loop runs — not a docs-only copy
oneFixtureTwoConsumers.rationale: #626 Fork 2: examples ARE webcases. One fixture serving both surfaces means a docs example can never drift from the case the conformance suite verifies.

isolationIsTheContract.description: The block's job is the isolation boundary, not the component being shown
isolationIsTheContract.rationale: A docs story must render an arbitrary component without its styles/scripts colliding with the docs chrome — isolation (iframe default, shadow-only opt-in) is the reusable concern; the component is just content.

presentationIsOptionalWebcaseFields.description: Ordering and prose ride as optional WebCase fields, not a second artifact
presentationIsOptionalWebcaseFields.rationale: Avoids a parallel docs-metadata file that would drift from the case; the most-flexible default is the bare {id,title,description,code} with presentation fields opt-in.
